steps.title: Substitute x = 6 into the function
steps.content:
We need to find $f(6)$. Replace $x$ with \$6$ in $f(x) = \frac{1}{2}(x + 12)$:
$$f(6) = \frac{1}{2}(6 + 12)$$

steps.title: Simplify the expression
steps.content: Add inside the parentheses: $6 + 12 = 18$. Then multiply by $\frac{1}{2}$: $$f(6) = \frac{1}{2} \cdot 18 = 9$$ That matches choice D — D is correct.
